phase1.output: (Window with animating shapes — rectangles moving, circles fading, squares rotating, etc.)
phase1.explanation:
Animation makes your UI dynamic and engaging:

Transition Types:

TranslateTransition:
• Moves node from one position to another
• setFromX() / setToX() — horizontal
• setFromY() / setToY() — vertical

FadeTransition:
• Changes opacity (fade in/out)
• setFromValue() / setToValue() (0.0 to 1.0)

RotateTransition:
• Rotates node
• setFromAngle() / setToAngle() (degrees)

ScaleTransition:
• Changes size
• setFromX() / setToX() — horizontal scale
• setFromY() / setToY() — vertical scale

Timeline:
• Custom animation with key frames
• KeyFrame at specific time
• Executes code at each frame

SequentialTransition:
• Plays animations in sequence
• One after another

ParallelTransition:
• Plays animations simultaneously

Animation controls:
• play() — start/continue
• pause() — pause playing
• stop() — stop and reset
• setCycleCount(n) — number of times to play
• setAutoReverse(true) — play forward then backward

Common properties:
• Duration.seconds(2) — duration in seconds
• Duration.millis(500) — duration in milliseconds

Animation status:
• RUNNING — currently playing
• PAUSED — paused
• STOPPED — stopped or not started

Use cases:
• Loading animations
• Transitions between screens
• Interactive feedback (hover effects)
• Games and visualizations

⚠️ Animation runs on the JavaFX Application Thread

phase2.errorMessage: (Rectangle appears but doesn't animate — no movement!)
phase2.explanation:
You created the animation but never called play()!

FIX: Add tt.play(); after configuring the animation.

Also consider:
• Animation only runs when play() is called
• Can also use playFromStart()
• Check if animation is playing: tt.getStatus() == Animation.Status.RUNNING

Always remember to call play() to start your animations!

phase3.prompt:
Write a JavaFX program with:
1. A Circle (radius 30) that moves horizontally across the screen
2. A Button "Start Animation" that starts the movement
3. A Button "Stop Animation" that stops and resets

Use TranslateTransition with duration 2 seconds, from X=0 to X=300.
The animation should auto-reverse and loop indefinitely.
dialogueHints: tt.play(); to start, tt.stop(); to stop
phase3.openingDialogue: Animation — bring your UI to life! 🎬
